title: Step 1: Setup
text:
Step 1.1 — Check Python
We need Python 3.10 or newer.

import sys

print("Python version:", sys.version)
assert sys.version_info >= (3, 10), "Need Python 3.10+"

Python version: 3.11.15 (main, Jun  2 2026, 22:26:03) [Clang 22.1.3 ]

title: Step 1: Setup
text:
Step 1.2 — Say hello to bird-watcher
Import the package and print its version.

import bird_watcher
print("bird-watcher version:", bird_watcher.__version__)

bird-watcher version: 0.0.0

title: Step 1: Setup
text:
Step 1.3 — Load config.yaml
We use config.yaml for local settings like the Slack webhook, your phone’s IP, and the Hugging Face token. The shared setup cell reads that file once so every notebook gets the same values.


load_environment
def load_environment(
    env_path:str='../.env', debug_prn:bool=False
):
Load environment variables from a .env file.



load_config
def load_config(
    config_path:str='../config.yaml', debug_prn:bool=False
):
Load configuration from a YAML file.

load_config(debug_prn=True)
load_environment( debug_prn=True)

loading config ../config.yaml
loading environment variables from ../.env


1



R

# From config.yaml (or hardcoded fallback)
SLACK_WEBHOOK = str(CONFIG.get("slack_webhook", ""))
HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY = str(CONFIG.get("huggingface_api_key", ""))

SNAPSHOT_FOLDER.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
CROP_FOLDER.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
print(f"Snapshot folder: {SNAPSHOT_FOLDER}")
print(f"Config file: {CONFIG_FILE}")
print(f"Phone URL: {PHONE_URL}")

title: Step 1: Setup
text:
Acceptance criterion
The three print lines below should show real values (not errors).

assert SNAPSHOT_FOLDER.exists(), f"{SNAPSHOT_FOLDER} should exist after the setup cell"
assert isinstance(PHONE_IP, str) and PHONE_IP, "PHONE_IP should be a non-empty string"
assert PHONE_URL.startswith("http"), "PHONE_URL should look like http://..."
print("✅ Setup complete")

✅ Setup complete
